qa_pairs.question: When is it not advisable to train a model on a GPU on your clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: It is not advisable to train a model on a GPU if it is fairly small and does not take up a large portion of GPU memory or use a reasonable amount of its compute capacity. In such cases, using one or more CPUs is recommended instead.

qa_pairs.question: Under what circumstances can Data Parallelism on a single GPU be a viable option for a small model?
qa_pairs.answer: Data Parallelism on a single GPU becomes a viable option for a small model when you have a very large dataset and wish to perform training with a small batch size.

qa_pairs.question: What is the benefit of using Data Parallelism with a single GPU for small models?
qa_pairs.answer: Using Data Parallelism allows fitting several replicas of a small model on a single GPU, which can increase resource usage and provide a good speed-up in training.

qa_pairs.question: Which tools are used to efficiently place multiple model replicas on a single GPU for Data Parallelism?
qa_pairs.answer: Nvidia's Multi-Process Service (MPS) along with MPI (Message Passing Interface) are used to efficiently place multiple model replicas on one GPU.
